items.title: Secretary-General of ASEAN attends the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference
items.summary: Secretary-General of ASEAN, Dr. Kao Kim Hourn, on 17 July 2026, attended the Opening Ceremony of the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC), in Shanghai, China. WAIC is a strategic platform to exchange perspectives on AI innovation, governance, and digital transformation, The post Secretary-General of ASEAN attends the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference appeared first on ASEAN Main Portal .

items.title: Policy paper: UK strategy on protection from sexual exploitation, abuse and harassment in international work
items.summary: This strategy sets out how the UK will prevent and respond to sexual exploitation, abuse and harassment linked to international work.

items.title: Policy paper: UK candidate for the International Court of Justice election 2026: Professor Dapo Akande, election brochure
items.summary: This election brochure for Professor Dapo Akande explains his experience and credentials as the UK candidate for election as a judge to the International Court of Justice for 2027 to 2036.

items.title: Policy paper: Fourth UK–Nigeria Security and Defence Partnership Dialogue, June 2026: communiqué
items.summary: The UK–Nigeria Security and Defence Partnership is a flagship pillar of the UK–Nigeria Strategic Partnership, deepening co-operation on shared security challenges across Nigeria and the wider region.

items.title: Twelfth anniversary of the downing of Flight MH17: Joint statement to the OSCE
items.summary: The UK and eight other countries marked twelve years since the downing of Flight MH17, stressing their unwavering commitment to truth, justice and accountability, noting international rulings attributing responsibility to Russia, and calling for full compliance with international law and reparation for its wrongful acts.

items.title: Secretary-General of ASEAN delivers Keynote Speech at the 2026 ASEAN-ROK Regional Connectivity Forum
items.summary: Secretary-General of ASEAN, Dr. Kao Kim Hourn, today delivered the keynote speech at the 2026 ASEAN-ROK Regional Connectivity Forum in Jakarta, Indonesia. items.title: Innovation under fire: Ukraine’s energy defense is a blueprint for the world
items.summary: Those who still treat energy resilience as a technical matter are not prepared for the changes currently taking place in military doctrine. Thanks to Ukraine, the blueprint to address this vulnerability exists, writes Oleksii Riabchyn. The post Innovation under fire: Ukraine’s energy defense is a blueprint for the world appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: European security strengthened under landmark UK and Estonia agreement
items.summary: European security will be strengthened by a new UK-Estonia defence roadmap that will deepen military cooperation, strengthen NATO’s eastern flank and modernise the UK’s Forward Land Forces presence in Estonia for the challenges of modern warfare.

items.title: Commission boosts Europe's competitiveness, decarbonisation and independence with Electrification Action Plan and ETS review
items.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 17 Jul 2026 The Commission today presents an Electrification Action Plan to make Europe the first electro-powered continent, and a stronger carbon market to support EU's industry in the clean transition and electrification.

items.title: 2026 Rule of Law Report strengthens Europe's prosperity, security and democratic resilience
items.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 17 Jul 2026 Today, the European Commission published its seventh annual Rule of Law Report, assessing the state of the rule of law across all EU Member States, as well as four candidate countries: Albania, Montenegro, North Macedonia and Serbia.

items.title: Drone superpower Ukraine is now Europe’s first line of defense
items.summary: Ukrainian troops took part in the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris for the first time on July 14, underling Ukraine's key role as Europe's first line of defense against Putin's Russia, writes David Kirichenko. The post Drone superpower Ukraine is now Europe’s first line of defense appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Kroenig quoted in Jiji on the NATO Summit
items.summary: On July 6, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was quoted in Jiji, a Japanese news outlet, on the NATO Summit in Ankara. The post Kroenig quoted in Jiji on the NATO Summit appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Israel’s next government must prioritize the Abraham Accords
items.summary: The fall election could open a narrow window for Israel to reset its regional strategy and create momentum to expand normalization. The post Israel’s next government must prioritize the Abraham Accords appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
items.summary: Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: How to implement a full maritime services ban on Russian oil
items.summary: The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price.
